title: Chesed (Loving-Kindness): The Trait That Sustains the World
title_zh: Chesed (חסד) — 仁爱:支撑世界的品德
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge
key_concepts: Chesed as voluntary kindness beyond justice
World stands on three things including chesed
Practical acts: bikur cholim, hachnasat orchim, nichum aveilim
Imitating God through acts of kindness

chinese_cultural_parallels.description: Confucian virtue of benevolence/humaneness, parallels Jewish chesed as foundation of ethical behavior
Filial piety, parallels Jewish commandment to honor parents and chesed toward elders as exemplified by Ruth
Propriety and ritual structuring social relationships, similar to how Jewish law codifies acts of chesed
Superior person in Confucian thought who embodies ren, parallels Jewish ideal of leaders excelling in chesed

practical_applications: Attend funerals and honor the deceased
Support brides and grooms preparing for marriage
Perform small daily acts of kindness
Combine financial support with personal presence
learning_outcomes: Understand the definition and scope of chesed
Recognize biblical models of chesed (Abraham, Ruth)
Appreciate chesed as one of three pillars sustaining the world
Identify practical acts of chesed in Jewish tradition
Connect Jewish chesed to Chinese cultural values (ren, xiao)
Develop personal strategies for cultivating chesed
Apply chesed principles to modern daily life
further_study: Genesis 18:1-8 - Abraham's hospitality to the angels
Book of Ruth - Chesed between family members across generations
Pirkei Avot 1:2 - The three pillars that sustain the world
Talmud Sotah 14a - Imitating God's ways through specific acts
Ramban's Commentary on Deuteronomy 6:18 - Doing what is right and good
Chofetz Chaim's works on loving-kindness and speech ethics
Messilat Yesharim (Path of the Just) - Rabbi Moshe Chaim Luzzatto on character development
